scope: Less-invasive implementation requested after rejecting the 16-wave H64xN64 owner: keep the existing 4-wave query-major score/prob/scaled-dP producer in source_boundary_query_major_scorepage_dparallel.hip and change only the dKV epilogue hook.

new_modes.7: direct head-wave contribution sink; bypasses the older shared-memory H16-partial reduce epilogue
new_modes.8: direct head-wave contribution atomicAdd to compressed dKV; bypasses the older shared-memory H16-partial reduce epilogue

mode2_score_grad_prob_sink.read: existing 4-wave score/prob/scaled-dP producer with no dKV contribution

mode4_shared_reduce_contribution_sink.read: same producer plus older shared-memory H16 partial reduce epilogue, checksum sink

mode3_shared_reduce_valid_dkv.read: same producer plus older shared-memory H16 partial reduce epilogue and valid dKV atomics

mode7_direct_contribution_sink.read: bypassing the shared-memory H16 partial reduce epilogue does not improve the contribution-sink path

mode8_direct_valid_dkv_atomic.read: direct head-wave partial atomics are worse than the older reduced-atomic epilogue

decision.root_cause: The less-invasive hook confirms the shared-memory H16 partial-reduce epilogue is not the local wall. Mode 7 is essentially equal to the old mode 4 sink, while mode 8 gets worse because it emits four head-wave partial atomics. The current 4-wave HIP/CK score-page producer is already slower than Triton, and the dKV contribution consumer attached to it adds about 14-15 ms before valid writes.
rejected: direct head-wave dKV epilogue as a speed path
optimizing the existing H16 partial-reduce epilogue in isolation
decision.next_code_step: Keep the 4-wave score/prob producer unchanged, but the next epilogue must be group-aware across multiple tokens or a persistent producer-consumer tile; a token_block=1 epilogue has route_atomic_reduction_x=1.0 and cannot realize the 23.8x route coalescing model.
